scope_boundary: deterministic resource-budget, generation-mode, and budget-ledger fixture accounting only; no budget scheduler, review-queue optimizer, load-stability experiment, KV-cache memory measurement, cost-quality economics result, verification-tax optimization, runtime budget enforcement, or serving benchmark
transition_reason: The repository now has resource-budget schema validation, generation-mode fixtures that require budget refs, useful-solution-per-second accounting, quality metrics, residual metrics, fallback behavior, and no-promotion boundaries, plus a deterministic budget-ledger harness for dispatch, high-risk escalation, protected overhead, displaced-cost residualization, scarce review-capacity deferral, and expected-invalid shortcut rejection. The Resource Economics chapter already frames budgets as typed records for value, risk, capacity, verification tax, protected gates, serving pressure, and residuals. These artifacts improve the record discipline for future budget tests, but they do not implement a budget scheduler, optimize a review queue, measure load stability, reproduce KV-cache memory behavior, quantify verification-tax optimization, or establish an economic outcome.
required_artifacts: budget allocator or scheduler run with task, route, capacity, and protected-gate records
load-stability scenario with burst, regeneration, deferral, and failure accounting
review-capacity allocation trace with actual high-risk and low-risk queue behavior
risk-adjusted verification-tax experiment with baseline and negative control
KV-cache or serving-memory measurement tied to resource-budget records
cost-quality tradeoff run with residual and human-review accounting
independent review of source interpretation adequacy
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
verification_command: python3 scripts/validate_generation_mode_baselines.py && python3 scripts/validate_resource_budget_ledgers.py && python3 scripts/validate_evidence_transitions.py && python3 scripts/validate_v1_status_snapshot.py
negative_results: resource-budget fixtures are deterministic accounting fixtures
budget-ledger fixtures are deterministic decision fixtures
no budget scheduler or allocator was run
no review-capacity queue optimizer was run
no load-stability or verification-tax experiment was run
no KV-cache, serving, or economic result is established
downgrade_triggers: chapter text interpreted as measured budget optimization
scarce review-capacity fixture interpreted as deployed queue behavior
KV-cache or serving context treated as reproduced local performance evidence
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to deterministic accounting behavior that passed, or after budget-allocation runs, review-capacity allocation traces, load-stability scenarios, cost-quality-residual measurements, serving-memory or KV-cache measurements, verification-tax experiments, and independent source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: deterministic fixtures do not implement a scheduler or allocator
scarce review-capacity fixtures do not optimize or measure a real review queue
schema validation does not prove load stability or budget optimality
external serving literature supplies comparison vocabulary rather than reproduced local measurements
verification tax remains a required accounting field, not an optimized result
no independent economic, serving, or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent scheduler, serving-system, economic, benchmarking, or source-interpretation audit
non_claims: does not promote Resource Economics above argument
does not prove KV-cache or serving performance
does not prove verification-tax optimization or economic outcomes
